speaker: ORLÉANS
text: The sun doth gild our armor. Up, my lords.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Montez à cheval! My horse, varlet! Lackey! Ha!

speaker: ORLÉANS
text: O brave spirit!

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Via les eaux et terre.

speaker: ORLÉANS
text: Rien puis? L’air et feu?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Cieux, cousin Orléans.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Now, my Lord Constable?

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Hark how our steeds for present service neigh.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Mount them, and make incision in their hides,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That their hot blood may spin in English eyes

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And dout them with superfluous courage. Ha!

speaker: RAMBURES
text: What, will you have them weep our horses’ blood?

speaker: RAMBURES
text: How shall we then behold their natural tears?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The English are embattled, you French peers.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: To horse, you gallant princes, straight to horse.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Do but behold yond poor and starvèd band,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: And your fair show shall suck away their souls,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Leaving them but the shales and husks of men.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: There is not work enough for all our hands,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Scarce blood enough in all their sickly veins

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: To give each naked curtal ax a stain,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: That our French gallants shall today draw out

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: And sheathe for lack of sport. Let us but blow on them,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: The vapor of our valor will o’erturn them.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: ’Tis positive against all exceptions, lords,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: That our superfluous lackeys and our peasants,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Who in unnecessary action swarm

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: About our squares of battle, were enough

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: To purge this field of such a hilding foe,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Though we upon this mountain’s basis by

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Took stand for idle speculation,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: But that our honors must not. What’s to say?

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: A very little little let us do,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: And all is done. Then let the trumpets sound

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: The tucket sonance and the note to mount,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: For our approach shall so much dare the field

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: That England shall couch down in fear and yield.

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Why do you stay so long, my lords of France?

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Yond island carrions, desperate of their bones,

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Ill-favoredly become the morning field.

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Their ragged curtains poorly are let loose,

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: And our air shakes them passing scornfully.

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Big Mars seems bankrupt in their beggared host

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: And faintly through a rusty beaver peeps.

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: The horsemen sit like fixèd candlesticks

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: With torch staves in their hand, and their poor jades

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Lob down their heads, drooping the hides and hips,

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: The gum down-roping from their pale dead eyes,

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: And in their pale dull mouths the gemeled bit

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Lies foul with chawed grass, still and motionless.

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: And their executors, the knavish crows,

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Fly o’er them all, impatient for their hour.

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: Description cannot suit itself in words

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: To demonstrate the life of such a battle

speaker: GRANDPRÉ
text: In life so lifeless, as it shows itself.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: They have said their prayers, and they stay for death.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Shall we go send them dinners and fresh suits,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And give their fasting horses provender,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And after fight with them?

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: I stay but for my guard. On, to the field!

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: I will the banner from a trumpet take

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: And use it for my haste. Come, come away.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: The sun is high, and we outwear the day.
